name: Racing Mind Sleep Onset
description: Difficulty falling asleep due to persistent mental chatter, worries, or an active mind that won't quiet down.
immediateAction: Try the 4-7-8 breathing technique combined with cognitive unload journaling.

name: Middle-of-Night Adrenaline Wake
description: Waking up in the middle of the night with difficulty returning to sleep, often with a surge of alertness or anxiety.
immediateAction: Use stimulus control - get out of bed if awake >20 minutes and do something calming until drowsy.

name: Tired But Wired Hyperarousal
description: Physically exhausted but mentally alert, unable to transition to sleep despite fatigue.
immediateAction: Try progressive muscle relaxation followed by NSDR/yoga nidra to bridge mind-body disconnect.

name: Bedtime Anticipatory Anxiety
description: Fear or dread about not being able to sleep, creating a self-fulfilling prophecy of sleeplessness.
immediateAction: Practice non-striving: accept that sleep may come gradually. Try chamomile tea and body scan meditation.

name: Body Tension Dominant Sleeplessness
description: Physical tension, restlessness, or somatic anxiety preventing sleep onset.
immediateAction: Try progressive muscle relaxation combined with magnesium glycinate.
